questions.question: What is the purpose of the HP OCI Tool cross-platform architecture specification?
questions.justification: The synthesis text states that the purpose is to refactor the existing WPF (.NET 4.6.1) tool to a .NET 8 cross-platform architecture, ensuring consistency and stability of the core business logic and native interop layer across Windows, Linux, and macOS.

questions.question: What is the layered architecture of the HP OCI Tool?
questions.justification: The synthesis text describes the layered architecture, including the UI Presentation Layer, Core Business Layer, and Native Implementation Layer, and the dependencies between them.

questions.question: What are the engineering hard rules defined for the HP OCI Tool project?
questions.justification: The synthesis text outlines three engineering hard rules: Struct definition specifications, Calling Convention, and a prohibition on runtime unload of native libraries.
